description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a raised stone wall forming the well shaft. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof covered in reddish-brown shingles, supported by two sturdy wooden posts on either side. A horizontal wooden beam spans the posts, and a winding mechanism with a rope is visible, suggesting a functional water-drawing system. The well sits on a square patch of green grass, providing a naturalistic setting. The stonework appears weathered and robust, while the wooden elements show a rustic, hand-crafted quality. The interior of the well is open and dark, indicating depth and the presence of air inside.
description: A rustic stone water well with a shingled wooden roof, supported by posts and a winding mechanism, set on a grassy patch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the shingled roof, wooden posts, and the circular stone well with the winding mechanism visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular profile of the roof and the stone base, with the rope mechanism extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the roof, posts, and stone well, but with less visibility of the winding mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the rope extending from the winding mechanism and the stone base.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof with shingled tiles, no solid cover over the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A rope and winding handle mechanism is present, attached to a horizontal wooden beam.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, supporting a wooden roof structure.
